{"key_event":"PF executive dominance amid debt default","reasoning":"Civil society remained capable of major checks, shown by the 2011 alternation and the defeat of Bill 10, but media closures, police restrictions, opposition harassment, cadre violence, and COVID-19 limits reduced its routine ability to constrain the state. State coercive and executive power increased through politicized institutions, infrastructure patronage, and emergency-style controls, but fiscal stress, corruption scandals, power shortages, and the 2020 sovereign default left overall state capacity only slightly below the previous decade.","society_change":-0.7,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":6.3}
